creator: gpalmer@FreeBSD.org
text:
On Fri, Jan 25, 2013 at 11:50:12PM +0000, Brett Wynkoop wrote:
> 
> >Number:         175600
> >Category:       misc
> >Synopsis:       FreeBSD 9.x missing for x86
> >Confidential:   no
> >Severity:       non-critical
> >Priority:       low
> >Responsible:    freebsd-bugs
> >State:          open
> >Quarter:        
> >Keywords:       
> >Date-Required:
> >Class:          sw-bug
> >Submitter-Id:   current-users
> >Arrival-Date:   Sat Jan 26 00:00:00 UTC 2013
> >Closed-Date:
> >Last-Modified:
> >Originator:     Brett Wynkoop
> >Release:        9 or 9.1
> >Organization:
> >Environment:
> NA
> >Description:
> Visiting ftp.freebsd.org seems to yield no ISO for the 9.x branch of FreeBSD for x86 systems.
> 
> lynx ftp://ftp.freebsd.org/pub/FreeBSD/releases/i386/ISO-IMAGES/
> 
> 
>                                 /pub/FreeBSD/releases/i386/ISO-IMAGES directory
> Current directory is /pub/FreeBSD/releases/i386/ISO-IMAGES
> 
>    Up to i386
> Mar 22  2010  Directory        7.3
> Feb 20  2011  Directory        7.4
> Jul 19  2010  Directory        8.1
> Feb 20  2011  Directory        8.2
> Apr 10  2012  Directory        8.3
> Dec 19  2005  text/plain       README.TXT  1Kb
> 
> >How-To-Repeat:
> lynx ftp://ftp.freebsd.org/pub/FreeBSD/releases/i386/ISO-IMAGES/
> >Fix:
> Put the 9.x ISO images for FreeBSD in their proper place.

Try

ftp://ftp.freebsd.org/pub/FreeBSD/releases/ISO-IMAGES/9.1

or

ftp://ftp.freebsd.org/pub/FreeBSD/releases/i386/i386/ISO-IMAGES/9.1

Or preferably use your local FreeBSD mirror

Regards,

Gary
